versets.trad: In that, indeed, is a sign for whoever fears the chastisement of the Hereafter — a Day for which mankind shall be gathered, a Day to be witnessed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes three days: the lost day (yesterday, no longer in your hand); the aimed-at day (tomorrow, of which you do not know whether it will come); the witnessed day (today, in which you are — exposed to vanishing). Profit from it for what avails.

versets.ar: وَمَا نُؤَخِّرُهُ إِلَّا لِأَجَلٍ مَّعْدُودٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā nuʾakhkhiruhu illā li-ajalin maʿdūdin
versets.trad: We delay it only until an appointed term.
versets.commentaire: The term, says Qushayrī, neither hastens nor delays. Terms run according to what the True knows and wills; no request advances or defers an instant when its term comes.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَأْتِ لَا تَكَلَّمُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ فَمِنْهُمْ شَقِيٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ
versets.tr: yawma yaʾti lā takallamu nafsun illā bi-idhnihi fa-minhum shaqiyyun wa-saʿīdun
versets.trad: On the day it comes, no soul shall speak except by His leave: some shall be wretched and some happy.
versets.commentaire: The shaqī, says Qushayrī, is he to whom deprivation has been apportioned in his state, and the saʿīd he who has been provided with faith in his outcome. Esoterically: the wretched is in the prison of his own calculation and forgetful of the running decree; the happy has returned from the darkness of calculation and gained the quality of contemplating the decree. The wretched thinks his obediences come from him; the happy has freed himself from the bondage of humanness and knows that all events belong to God.

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ شَقُوا فَفِي النَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌ وَشَهِيقٌ
versets.tr: fa-ammā lladhīna shaqū fa-fī n-nāri lahum fīhā zafīrun wa-shahīqun
versets.trad: As for those who became wretched, they shall be in the Fire, with sighing and sobbing therein;
versets.commentaire: Zafīr and shahīq: the convulsed exhalation and inhalation of the condemned. The very breath, in the Fire, becomes torture.

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ السَّمَاوَاتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ رَبُّكَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ فَعَّالٌ لِّمَا يُرِيدُ
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā mā dāmati s-samāwātu wa-l-arḍu illā mā shāʾa rabbuka inna rabbaka faʿʿālun li-mā yurīdu
versets.trad: They shall abide there as long as the heavens and earth endure, save what your Lord wills: truly your Lord does what He wills.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the exception illā mā shāʾa rabbuk in several senses: save what your Lord wills to add to the duration of heavens and earth; or to move them to another form of chastisement besides the groan and sob; or to exempt them at some times — no, it is not an exemption at some of their times; or finally, to bring the people of tawḥīd out of the Fire, so that their wretchedness is not perpetual.

mots.racine: ف ع ل / ر و د

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا فَفِي الْجَنَّةِ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ السَّمَاوَاتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ رَبُّكَ ۖ عَطَاءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna suʿidū fa-fī l-jannati khālidīna fīhā mā dāmati s-samāwātu wa-l-arḍu illā mā shāʾa rabbuka ʿaṭāʾan ghayra majdhūdhin
versets.trad: As for those who became happy, they shall be in the Garden, abiding there as long as the heavens and earth endure, save what your Lord wills — a gift unbroken.
versets.commentaire: For them, says Qushayrī, today the gardens of nearness, tomorrow the gardens of reward; the disbelievers today in the chastisement of separation, tomorrow in the chastisement of burning. “A gift unbroken”: proof that these favors are neither cut off nor refused.

versets.ar: فَلَا تَكُ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِّمَّا يَعْبُدُ هَٰؤُلَاءِ ۚ مَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُهُم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ وَإِنَّا لَمُوَفُّوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ غَيْرَ مَنقُوصٍ
versets.tr: fa-lā taku fī miryatin mimmā yaʿbudu hāʾulāʾi mā yaʿbudūna illā kamā yaʿbudu ābāʾuhum min qablu wa-innā la-muwaffūhum naṣībahum ghayra manqūṣin
versets.trad: Be not in doubt about what these worship: they worship only as their fathers worshipped before. We shall surely pay them their portion in full, not diminished.
versets.commentaire: He does not mean that he would be in doubt, says Qushayrī, but it is verification of their imitating their fathers, as one says: “No doubt, this is day”. The address is to the prophet, but it aims at his community.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ فَاخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba fa-khtulifa fīhi wa-lawlā kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika la-quḍiya baynahum wa-innahum la-fī shakkin minhu murībin
versets.trad: We gave Moses the Book, and there arose disagreement about it. Were it not for a word that preceded from your Lord, judgment would have been passed between them; and they remain in disquieting doubt about it.
versets.commentaire: Benefit of the verse, says Qushayrī: relief for the Elect — ﷺ — in what he met of his people’s denial; hearing the story of the like, and of their like, is consolation. “O our neighbor, we are two strangers here, and every stranger is kin to the stranger.”

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ كُلًّا لَّمَّا لَيُوَفِّيَنَّهُمْ رَبُّكَ أَعْمَالَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-inna kullan lammā la-yuwaffiyannahum rabbuka aʿmālahum innahu bimā yaʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: Your Lord will surely give every one of them the exact recompense for their deeds. He is fully aware of what they do.
versets.commentaire: Recompense for deeds, says Qushayrī, is sometimes immediate, sometimes deferred. Whoever turns from heedlessness to the quality of vigilance finds, in his dealings, gain in the immediate and increase in the deferred. And what man finds in himself is more complete than what he attains by the science of manifest proofs.

mots.sens: He shall pay them in full

versets.ar: فَاسْتَقِمْ كَمَا أُمِرْتَ وَمَن تَابَ مَعَكَ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا ۚ إِنَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: fa-staqim kamā umirta wa-man tāba maʿaka wa-lā taṭghaw innahu bimā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: So stand firm as you are commanded — you and those who have turned with you — and do not transgress. Truly He sees what you do.
versets.commentaire: The verse that turned the Prophet’s hair white. Qushayrī unfolds: the sīn of istiqāma may be the sīn of asking — ask of God the station of uprightness. The truth of uprightness in obedience is perseverance in fulfilling its right without failing. — Uprightness of souls: the abolishing of the lapse. Uprightness of hearts: the abolishing of heedlessness. Uprightness of spirits: the abolishing of attachment. Uprightness of secrets: the abolishing of self-regard. Uprightness of worshippers: not to reserve themselves from worship; not to neglect its discharge; to complete its hard and its easy. Uprightness of renouncers: to hope for nothing of the world, little or much. Uprightness of penitents: not to brush near the pain of a fault, leaving the small and the great.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَرْكَنُوا إِلَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا فَتَمَسَّكُمُ النَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَاءَ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tarkanū ilā lladhīna ẓalamū fa-tamassakumu n-nāru wa-mā lakum min dūni llāhi min awliyāʾa thumma lā tunṣarūna
versets.trad: And do not lean toward the wrongdoers, or the Fire would touch you. You have no protectors besides God, then you would not be helped.
themes: do not lean toward the unjust
versets.commentaire: Not to lean, says Qushayrī, means: not to do their acts, nor approve them, nor praise them in their acts, nor abandon to them the command of good, nor take anything of their unlawful goods, nor dwell with them in your hearts, nor mix with them, nor consort with them — all this falls under the injunction.

versets.ar: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِّنَ اللَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ذِكْرَىٰ لِلذَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-aqimi ṣ-ṣalāta ṭarafayi n-nahāri wa-zulafan mina l-layli inna l-ḥasanāti yudhhibna s-sayyiʾāti dhālika dhikrā li-dh-dhākirīna
versets.trad: Establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approaches of the night. Truly, good deeds drive away evil ones. This is a reminder for those who remember.
themes: the two ends of the day
versets.commentaire: “Seize every instant by worship,” says Qushayrī. And he declines the “good deeds”: good deeds are what the True bestows, evils what the servant commits; when His favors enter upon the servant’s uglinesses, they efface them. — The good of nearness effaces the faults of the lapse. — The good of regret effaces the evils of crime. — The pouring of tears effaces the slip. — The good of gnosis effaces the evils of disobedience. — The good of pardon to brothers effaces rancor. — The good of sincerity effaces self-satisfaction. — The good of pure intent effaces the evils of ostentation.

mots.racine: ط ر ف / ن ه ر
mots.sens: the two ends of the day

versets.ar: وَاصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ṣbir fa-inna llāha lā yuḍīʿu ajra l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: And be patient; God does not let the wage of those who do good go to waste.
versets.commentaire: Patience, says Qushayrī, is to drink the cups of the decree without grimace, and the fair welcoming of the embrace of command and separation from the forbidden. The muḥsin is the actor who knows that the reward of patience and obedience is by His grace — Subḥānahu — not by the merit of any deed.

versets.ar: فَلَوْلَا كَانَ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ أُولُو بَقِيَّةٍ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْفَسَادِ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّنْ أَنجَيْنَا مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَاتَّبَعَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مَا أُتْرِفُوا فِيهِ وَكَانُوا مُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lawlā kāna mina l-qurūni min qablikum ulū baqiyyatin yanhawna ʿani l-fasādi fī l-arḍi illā qalīlan mimman anjaynā minhum wa-ttabaʿa lladhīna ẓalamū mā utrifū fīhi wa-kānū mujrimīna
versets.trad: Why were there not among the generations before you possessors of virtue forbidding corruption on earth — except for a few of those We saved? But the wrongdoers followed the easy life that had been given them, and were criminals.
versets.commentaire: The sense, says Qushayrī: there were among those before you, communities, only few men who forbade corruption, kept the religion, and obeyed their prophets — save a small number.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ لِيُهْلِكَ الْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُهَا مُصْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna rabbuka li-yuhlika l-qurā bi-ẓulmin wa-ahluhā muṣliḥūna
versets.trad: Your Lord would not destroy the towns unjustly while their inhabitants were doers of right.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī offers several levels of the muṣliḥ: he who fulfills the right of his Lord without claiming his share; he who prefers his salvation to his ruin; muṣliḥ whose obedience restores the soul; muṣliḥ whose knowledge of the Master restores the heart; muṣliḥ whose contemplation of the Master restores the secret.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شَاءَ رَبُّكَ لَجَعَلَ النَّاسَ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً ۖ وَلَا يَزَالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ
versets.tr: wa-law shāʾa rabbuka la-jaʿala n-nāsa ummatan wāḥidatan wa-lā yazālūna mukhtalifīna
versets.trad: Had your Lord willed, He would have made mankind one community; but they keep on diverging,
versets.commentaire: Had He willed, says Qushayrī, He would have made them masters of accord — without their adding anything to the splendor of His kingdom; and had He willed, He would have made them masters of divergence — without their inflicting any ugliness on Him. Divergence is according to His will.

mots.racine: أ م م / و ح د

versets.ar: إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ رَبُّكَ ۚ وَلِذَٰلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ۗ وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: illā man raḥima rabbuka wa-li-dhālika khalaqahum wa-tammat kalimatu rabbika la-amlaʾanna jahannama mina l-jinnati wa-n-nāsi ajmaʿīna
versets.trad: except those to whom your Lord has shown mercy. For that He created them. And the word of your Lord shall be fulfilled: “I shall surely fill Hell with jinn and men together.”
versets.commentaire: Save those to whom your Lord has shown mercy, says Qushayrī, by His prior decree: He preserved them from divergence in the outcome of their affairs, established them for Him, set them up for Him, fixed them in accord, love, and tawḥīd.

mots.racine: ت م م / ك ل م

versets.ar: وَكُلًّا نَّقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنبَاءِ الرُّسُلِ مَا نُثَبِّتُ بِهِ فُؤَادَكَ ۚ وَجَاءَكَ فِي هَٰذِهِ الْحَقُّ وَمَوْعِظَةٌ وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kullan naquṣṣu ʿalayka min anbāʾi r-rusuli mā nuthabbitu bihi fuʾādaka wa-jāʾaka fī hādhihi l-ḥaqqu wa-mawʿiẓatun wa-dhikrā li-l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: All that We recount to you of the messengers’ tidings is to make firm your heart. The truth has come to you in this, with an exhortation and a reminder for the believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He calmed his heart by what He recounted to him of the messengers, and let him know that He has raised no one to the degree He has raised him, nor granted to any what He has granted him. More subtly: the heart’s firmness is held less by what is recounted than by Him who recounts — there is a difference between understanding by what one hears and resting in Him from whom one hears.

versets.ar: وَقُل لِّلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ اعْمَلُوا عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنَّا عَامِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qul lilladhīna lā yuʾminūna ʿmalū ʿalā makānatikum innā ʿāmilūna
versets.trad: Say to those who do not believe: “Act according to your position; we are also acting.
versets.commentaire: The clean division: Qushayrī recalls that those who deny tawḥīd and prefer to the True other than the True are on the brink of being inundated by vengeance; they shall plunge into the seas of chastisement and collapse into the depths of humiliation.

versets.ar: وَانتَظِرُوا إِنَّا مُنتَظِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ntaẓirū innā muntaẓirūna
versets.trad: And wait; we too are waiting.”
versets.commentaire: The waiting, on both sides: for the one the opening of the True, for the other the fall of the decree.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُ الْأَمْرُ كُلُّهُ فَاعْبُدْهُ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَيْهِ ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lillāhi ghaybu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-ilayhi yurjaʿu l-amru kulluhu fa-ʿbudhu wa-tawakkal ʿalayhi wa-mā rabbuka bi-ghāfilin ʿammā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: To God belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth, and to Him is all matter returned. So worship Him and rely upon Him. Your Lord is not heedless of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Seal of the sūra. Qushayrī condenses: He has veiled the outcomes from hearts; He has hidden the antecedents from their sight; He has bound them to stand by what He has charged them with in the present. Hence “worship Him.” And if the heart is divided, if conjecture wavers, if one fears an evil outcome: “rely on Him” — that is, ward off the trial from yourself by the fair thought, the fair hope, the perdurance of expectation. “Your Lord is not heedless”: He encompasses all things in knowledge, and accomplishes in every matter His judgment.
